topic: A chef preparing Hand-Pulled Lanzhou Ramen (Lamian).
raw_generation:
{
  "script": "Scene 1 - Opening. A traditional lamian kitchen. The master chef steps into frame, flour in the air and a pot of broth bubbling. On screen text: Hand-Pulled Lanzhou Ramen Lamian.
Scene 2 - Dough. Close up on flour, water, and salt being measured. The chef kneads the dough until smooth, then covers it to rest for 20 to 30 minutes.
Scene 3 - Hand pulling. The rested dough is rolled into a long rope, folded, and slowly pulled into dozens of thin noodles, then laid on the bench and separated into strands.
Scene 4 - Broth. Beef bones simmer with ginger, scallions, and pepper. The broth clears and concentrates after hours of slow simmering.
Scene 5 - Assembly. The noodles are boiled briefly, then slid into a hot bowl. Top with thin beef slices, radish, cilantro, and scallions. Finish with chili oil and sesame oil.
Scene 6 - Finish. The chef tastes, gives tips on timing and texture, and invites the viewer to try making lamian at home.",
  "narration": "Welcome to the art of hand pulled lamian. In Lanzhou, a clear beef broth meets springy noodles pulled by hand. Today we walk through the core steps. First, prepare the dough with flour, water and a touch of salt, then let it rest to relax the gluten. Next, roll and hand pull the dough into long elastic strands. The broth is built by simmering beef bones with aromatics until clear and deeply savory. Boil the noodles briefly, then place them into the hot broth and crown with beef slices, radish, cilantro and scallions. A drizzle of chili oil and a kiss of sesame oil finish the bowl. Serve immediately and enjoy the balance of texture, aroma and heat."
}

parsed_generation.script:
Scene 1 - Opening. A traditional lamian kitchen. The master chef steps into frame, flour in the air and a pot of broth bubbling. On screen text: Hand-Pulled Lanzhou Ramen Lamian.
Scene 2 - Dough. Close up on flour, water, and salt being measured. The chef kneads the dough until smooth, then covers it to rest for 20 to 30 minutes.
Scene 3 - Hand pulling. The rested dough is rolled into a long rope, folded, and slowly pulled into dozens of thin noodles, then laid on the bench and separated into strands.
Scene 4 - Broth. Beef bones simmer with ginger, scallions, and pepper. The broth clears and concentrates after hours of slow simmering.
Scene 5 - Assembly. The noodles are boiled briefly, then slid into a hot bowl. Top with thin beef slices, radish, cilantro, and scallions. Finish with chili oil and sesame oil.
Scene 6 - Finish. The chef tastes, gives tips on timing and texture, and invites the viewer to try making lamian at home.
parsed_generation.narration: Welcome to the art of hand pulled lamian. In Lanzhou, a clear beef broth meets springy noodles pulled by hand. Today we walk through the core steps. First, prepare the dough with flour, water and a touch of salt, then let it rest to relax the gluten. Next, roll and hand pull the dough into long elastic strands. The broth is built by simmering beef bones with aromatics until clear and deeply savory. Boil the noodles briefly, then place them into the hot broth and crown with beef slices, radish, cilantro and scallions. A drizzle of chili oil and a kiss of sesame oil finish the bowl. Serve immediately and enjoy the balance of texture, aroma and heat.

feedback: The output stays on topic and follows a clear, action-first sequence. Consider explicit time cues per step and ensure each shot centers on a single action.
